title: Umami Light Ukrainian Chicken Soup
description: A light, kid-friendly Ukrainian chicken soup brimming with umami from ancho chilies, perfect for diabetics thanks to low sugar and balanced macros.
instructions: Prepare the vegetables: dice the carrot, celery, and onion; mince the garlic.
In a large pot, bring 1.5 liters of water to a gentle boil.
Add the chicken breast pieces, reduce heat to low, and poach until cooked through (about 15 minutes).
Remove the chicken, let it cool slightly, then shred into bite‑size pieces. Set aside.
Return the pot to low heat; add the diced carrot, celery, onion, minced garlic, and ancho chili powder. Sauté for 3–4 minutes until the vegetables soften.
Stir in the tomato paste (if using) and a splash of water to create a light broth base.
Add the shredded chicken back to the pot, season with salt, pepper, and fresh dill, and simmer gently for 5 minutes so the flavors meld.
Taste and adjust seasoning, adding more pepper or dill if desired.
Optional: finish with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice to brighten the soup.
Serve hot in bowls, garnishing with extra dill if desired.
